text: Watch memory usage per process: `ps aux --sort=-%mem | head` (or run it twice a few seconds apart) -- one process's RSS should be visibly climbing while everything else stays flat.
That process is `cache-warmer`. Confirm it's the one growing: `ps -o pid,rss,cmd -C cache-warmer`, then check again a few seconds later -- the RSS number goes up each time.
It's allocating memory and never freeing it, so there's no 'wait it out' fix -- stop it: `sudo pkill -f cache-warmer`.
Confirm it's gone and memory usage has stopped climbing: `pgrep -f cache-warmer` should return nothing, and `free -m` should hold steady afterward.
